findings.evidence: Existing image API is bundled as `def range (f : Hom R C M N) : Subcomodule R C N := (⊤ : Subcomodule R C M).map f` with `range_toSubmodule`, `mem_range`, and `range_le_iff` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcomodule.lean:332-359`; new grep hits show only `comap_bot_toSubmodule` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcomodule/Comap.lean:169-172` and no `Comodule.Hom.ker`.
findings.fix: Add `Comodule.Hom.ker := (⊥ : Subcomodule R C N).comap f` with `@[simp] ker_toSubmodule`, `@[simp] mem_ker`, and the usual kernel universal property/zero-membership lemmas so downstream code need not spell kernels as a bottom comap.
findings.issue: The PR identifies kernels as a downstream use but exposes only `((⊥).comap f).toSubmodule = LinearMap.ker f.toLinearMap`; there is no bundled `Comodule.Hom.ker` subcomodule API analogous to the existing `Comodule.Hom.range`.

summary: The inverse-image API now has the basic comap characterizations and the Galois connection, but it still leaves the kernel-facing public surface incomplete relative to the existing range API and the stated downstream kernel use.
